menu_001.name: Jollof Rice
menu_001.description: Smoky, richly seasoned Nigerian jollof rice cooked with plum tomatoes, peppers, and a blend of aromatic spices.

menu_002.name: Fried Rice
menu_002.description: Stir-fried rice with mixed vegetables, eggs, and a savoury seasoning blend, served Nigerian-style.

menu_003.name: White Rice
menu_003.description: Perfectly steamed long-grain white rice, a versatile accompaniment to any protein or sauce.

menu_004.name: Coconut Rice
menu_004.description: Fragrant rice slow-cooked in fresh coconut milk with a hint of bay leaf and seasoning.

menu_005.name: Pasta
menu_005.description: Al dente pasta tossed in a rich tomato-based Nigerian sauce with assorted vegetables and seasoning.

menu_006.name: Beans
menu_006.description: Slowly cooked honey beans in a spiced palm oil base — a classic Nigerian comfort dish.

menu_007.name: Moi Moi
menu_007.description: Steamed bean pudding blended with onions, peppers, and seasoning. A beloved Nigerian staple.

menu_008.name: Grandeur Full Breakfast
menu_008.description: Eggs your way, grilled tomatoes, sautéed mushrooms, crispy turkey bacon, toasted sourdough, and freshly squeezed orange juice.

menu_009.name: Akara
menu_009.description: Crispy deep-fried bean cakes seasoned with pepper and onion — a classic Nigerian breakfast favourite.

menu_010.name: Pap (Akamu)
menu_010.description: Smooth, lightly fermented cornmeal porridge served warm, often paired with akara or moi moi.

menu_011.name: Egusi Soup
menu_011.description: Rich, aromatic ground melon seed soup slow-cooked with assorted meats, smoked fish, and leafy vegetables.

menu_012.name: Ogbono Soup
menu_012.description: Draw soup made from ground ogbono seeds, palm oil, and assorted proteins — full-bodied and deeply satisfying.

menu_013.name: Pepper Soup
menu_013.description: A light, fiery broth of choice protein simmered with native pepper soup spices. Available with fish, goat, or chicken.

menu_014.name: Ofe Onugbu (Bitter Leaf Soup)
menu_014.description: An Igbo classic — bitter leaf and cocoyam-thickened soup with assorted meat and stockfish.

menu_015.name: Grilled Chicken
menu_015.description: Whole chicken leg marinated in suya-spiced blend, char-grilled and served with sliced onions and tomato.

menu_016.name: Fried Chicken
menu_016.description: Tender, seasoned chicken pieces coated in a light batter and deep-fried to golden perfection.

menu_017.name: Beef
menu_017.description: Slow-cooked tender beef in a spiced broth, finished in the oven for a satisfying caramelised exterior.

menu_018.name: Fish
menu_018.description: Fresh whole fish seasoned with native spices and either grilled or fried. Available in tilapia or mackerel.

menu_019.name: Plantain
menu_019.description: Ripe plantain slices fried to a golden caramel finish — sweet, soft, and utterly irresistible.

menu_020.name: Salad
menu_020.description: Fresh garden salad with lettuce, cucumber, tomatoes, carrots, and a choice of dressing.

menu_021.name: Pounded Yam
menu_021.description: Silky hand-pounded yam — the perfect complement to any Grandeur soup.

menu_022.name: Eba (Garri)
menu_022.description: Smooth cassava-based swallow, a staple of Nigerian cuisine, best paired with any of our soups.

menu_023.name: Semolina
menu_023.description: Finely milled semolina swallow — smooth, light, and ideal with any of our signature soups.

menu_024.name: Amala
menu_024.description: Dark, velvety yam flour swallow — a Yoruba classic that pairs beautifully with ewedu or gbegiri.

menu_025.name: Chapman
menu_025.description: Nigeria's beloved party cocktail — a chilled blend of Fanta, Sprite, Ribena, and Angostura bitters over ice.

menu_026.name: Zobo Royal
menu_026.description: Hibiscus flowers steeped with ginger, cloves, and pineapple — refreshingly served over crushed ice.

menu_027.name: Fresh Juice
menu_027.description: Freshly pressed seasonal fruit juice. Ask your server for today's selection.

menu_028.name: Water
menu_028.description: Chilled still or sparkling mineral water.
